resources.string: Shape app screens directly on Android
Build a concept quickly with reusable blocks, live preview, editable layers, and a portable JSON spec. No backend involved.
Long-press a block, drag it onto the phone, and drop it where the screen should place it.
Drag blocks in from the palette. Drag existing blocks freely; long-press a block to show resize markers.
Tap a layer to edit it. Position and size are controlled directly on the phone preview.
Drag the selected block directly on the phone preview. Long-press it to show resize markers.
Choose a name for this design. It will be stored in the app private files on this device.
A saved project with this name already exists. Replace it?
Long-press a palette block and drop it on the phone preview.
Drag blocks from the palette into the phone preview. Save and load projects locally, or export the design as JSON.
Long-press a component type and drop it on the phone preview.
Use Start new for a blank design, or choose a real-life preset from the dropdown.
Tap a component to edit it. Drag it freely on the canvas. Long-press it to show edge and corner resize markers.
Tap a layer to select it. Delete removes the selected layer.
Tap a widget or layer to open the side inspector drawer. Edit content, colors, width, height, text size, padding, corners, alignment, and style.

1. Name your project at the top.

2. Turn on Dark skin if you want the builder interface in a dark theme.

3. Start from scratch with Start new, or choose a real-life preset from the preset dropdown.

4. Add elements by long-pressing a palette item, then dragging it onto the phone preview.

5. Move a widget by dragging its body inside the phone mockup.

6. Resize a widget by long-pressing it. Yellow resize handles appear around it. Drag side handles for width or height, and corner handles for both.

7. Select a widget by tapping it or tapping it in Layers. Its attributes open in the side inspector drawer.

8. Edit title, helper text, colors, width, height, text size, padding, corner radius, alignment, and style.

9. Save stores the project locally on this device. Load opens saved projects. Export copies the design as JSON.

10. If something fails, send Downloads/uidesignerFailLog.txt.
